steps.title: Spot the cyclical pattern
steps.content: The claim describes an ongoing cycle: anticipation when new years arrive, followed by regretful reflection as they depart. The key signal is that this pattern repeats—it's not a single occurrence but a recurring sequence the speaker experiences again and again. The right quotation must capture both the hopeful forward-looking and the sorrowful backward-looking as part of this repetitive cycle.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the pattern
steps.content: D embodies the complete cycle: “each, coming, brings ambitions high” shows the anticipatory hope, “each, departing, leaves a sigh linked to the past” conveys the regretful reflection, and “the years go swiftly by” emphasizes the ongoing, repetitive nature. A, B, and C each lack essential components—either the anticipation, the reflection, or the cyclical repetition. That matches choice D — D is correct.
